ddls:
CREATE TABLE "services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service */,
  "service_type" TEXT /* Type of service (e.g., authentication, data storage) */,
  "endpoint" TEXT /* Endpoint URL of the service */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the service (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the service was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the service was last updated */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the service */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the service */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the service type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the service type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "endpoints" (
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each endpoint */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service the endpoint belongs to */,
  "endpoint_url" TEXT /* URL of the endpoint */,
  "method" TEXT /* HTTP method supported by the endpoint (e.g., GET, POST) */,
  "path" TEXT /* Path of the endpoint */,
  "query_params" TEXT /* Query parameters of the endpoint */,
  "header_params" TEXT /* Header parameters of the endpoint */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("endpoint_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_endpoints_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_metadata" (
  "metadata_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metadata entry */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service the metadata belongs to */,
  "key" TEXT /* Key of the metadata (e.g., version, author) */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the metadata */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the metadata (e.g., performance, security) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metadata_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service the request was made to */,
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* ID of the endpoint the request was made to */,
  "caller_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the caller */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the request */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the request (e.g., success, failure) */,
  "reject_reason" TEXT /* Reason for rejection (if any) */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action taken (if any) */,
  "request_body" TEXT /* Request body of the HTTP request */,
  "response_body" TEXT /* Response body of the HTTP response */,
  "response_code" INTEGER /* HTTP response code */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_endpoint_id FOREIGN KEY ("endpoint_id") REFERENCES endpoints ("endpoint_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_metadata" (
  "metadata_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metadata entry */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the request the metadata belongs to */,
  "key" TEXT /* Key of the metadata (e.g., user_agent, referer) */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the metadata */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the metadata (e.g., authentication, authorization) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metadata_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_metadata_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES requests ("request_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, developer) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the service */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service being accessed */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the access */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action taken (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action taken (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics entry */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service the analytics belongs to */,
  "metric" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., request_count, error_rate) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the analytics entry */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of metric (e.g., count, average, sum) */,
  "dimension" TEXT /* Dimension of the metric (e.g., time, location) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction entry */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service the prediction belongs to */,
  "metric" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., request_count, error_rate) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the prediction entry */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of machine learning model used */,
  "confidence" REAL /* Confidence level of the prediction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service related to the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message of the notification */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the notification entry */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., email, SMS) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the notification (e.g., sent, read) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "revisions" (
  "revision_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each revision entry */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service the revision belongs to */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the revision */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the revision entry */,
  "revision_type" TEXT /* Type of revision (e.g., update, delete) */,
  "author" TEXT /* Author of the revision */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("revision_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_revisions_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "security_incidents" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident entry */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service related to the incident */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the incident */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the incident entry */,
  "incident_type" TEXT /* Type of incident (e.g., unauthorized access, data breach) */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the incident (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_incidents_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)
